user_query:
I'm planning a public-facing fall outreach series on the North Caucasian Imamate and need a structured content calendar I can hand to my department’s communications team.

Please use the provided starter files to generate a 12-week plan that schedules two items per week across our 'blog' and 'newsletter' channels. The plan should start on 2024-09-02 and publish on Mondays and Thursdays.

What I need you to do:
- Inspect the scripts/ and input/ directories to understand the expected data formats and configuration keys.
- From the workspace root, run: python3 scripts/plan_generator.py
  • The first run will fail because config/config.json is incomplete. Analyze the error message, then fix config/config.json without changing the script so that publishing occurs on Mondays and Thursdays. Specifically, add the missing publish_days key as [0,3] (0=Mon..6=Sun). Keep all other values as provided.
- Re-run the script until it completes successfully and produces both outputs.

Deliverables and constraints:
- Write the outputs to:
  • output/content_calendar.csv
  • output/content_calendar.json
- The calendar must cover exactly 12 weeks from 2024-09-02 with two items per week (24 total entries) distributed across channels by alternating blog → newsletter → blog → newsletter, etc.
- Each CSV row and each JSON object must include exactly these fields: week, publish_date, channel, topic_id, title, primary_tag, audience, keywords.
  • In CSV, keywords should be a semicolon-separated string; in JSON, keywords should be an array.
  • publish_date values must fall on Mondays and Thursdays beginning 2024-09-02.
  • primary_tag should be the first tag from the inventory for that topic.
  • If a topic lacks keywords in the inventory, use the fallback keywords from the config plus the primary_tag.
- Save a brief run log to output/run_log.txt that captures (1) the initial error line from the failed run and (2) the final success line showing where the outputs were written.

Input paths to use:
- scripts/plan_generator.py (starter script)
- config/config.json (initially missing publish_days)
- input/inventory.csv (topic inventory)

Please do not hardcode new data; work with the provided inputs, fix the configuration, and produce the specified outputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
